bibliographic_data.title: On Preserving the Behavior in Software Refactoring: A Systematic Mapping Study
bibliographic_data.abstract: Context: Refactoring is the art of modifying the design of a system without altering its behavior. The idea is to reorganize variables, classes and methods to facilitate their future adaptations and comprehension. As the concept of behavior preservation is fundamental for refactoring, several studies, using formal verification, language transformation and dynamic analysis, have been proposed to monitor the execution of refactoring operations and their impact on the program semantics. However, there is no existing study that examines the available behavior preservation strategies for each refactoring operation. Objective: This paper identifies behavior preservation approaches in the research literature. Method: We conduct, in this paper, a systematic mapping study, to capture all existing behavior preservation approaches that we classify based on several criteria including their methodology, applicability, and their degree of automation. Results: The results indicate that several behavior preservation approaches have been proposed in the literature. The approaches vary between using formalisms and techniques, developing automatic refactoring safety tools, and performing a manual analysis of the source code. Conclusion: Our taxonomy reveals that there exist some types of refactoring operations whose behavior preservation is under-researched. Our classification also indicates that several possible strategies can be combined to better detect any violation of the program semantics.

core_content.novelty_claim: The paper identifies and classifies behavior preservation approaches in software refactoring, highlighting under-researched areas and potential strategies for ensuring safe refactorings.
core_content.key_methodology: A systematic mapping study was conducted to capture all existing behavior preservation approaches based on several criteria including methodology, applicability, and degree of automation.
outcomes: Several behavior preservation approaches have been proposed in the literature.
The approaches vary between using formalisms and techniques, developing automatic refactoring safety tools, and performing a manual analysis of the source code.
Some types of refactoring operations are under-researched for behavior preservation.
core_content.full_text_markdown: This paper addresses the challenge of preserving software behavior during refactoring. Refactoring is defined as optimizing the internal structure of the code without changing its external behavior, and several studies have proposed methods to monitor this process. However, no existing study has comprehensively examined the strategies for each type of refactoring operation. The authors conducted a systematic mapping study to identify and classify these approaches based on methodology, applicability, and degree of automation. They found that while various techniques exist, some types of refactorings are under-researched. This work aims to provide researchers and practitioners with insights into existing behavior preservation strategies and highlight areas for future research.
